name: codex-approval-never-safe-git-allow
doc: Codex `approval_policy=never` (AskForApproval::Never) has no interactive ask channel — Codex maps it to `permission_mode:"bypassPermissions"` on the hook payload (codex-rs/core/src/hook_runtime.rs::hook_permission_mode; NOT a config.toml read — a CLI override could contradict the on-disk file). handle-pre.ts sets neverApproval only for id==="codex" + Bash + that exact string. evaluate.ts's 3-condition exemption (RALPH_SAFE subset, not GIT_BLOCKED, not chained) turns the usual GIT_ASK `ask` into an auto-approved `warn` — a non-blocking systemMessage, never a permissionDecision.

steps.doc: `git commit` is in RALPH_SAFE and not destructive/chained → neverApproval auto-approves with a visible systemMessage. NO permissionDecision at all (non-blocking) — the agent-facing additionalContext carries the same auto-approve note.
`git push --force` matches GIT_BLOCKED — the neverApproval exemption explicitly requires !GIT_BLOCKED, so this stays a hard deny exactly like scenario 19/23, even under approval_policy=never.

A plain `git push` is NOT in RALPH_SAFE — neverApproval does not auto-approve it. It still hits GIT_ASK, downgraded to deny on Codex exactly like scenario 23/26 — the exemption never widens the RALPH_SAFE subset itself, only what happens INSIDE it.

Anti-chaining regression: `git commit -m x && git push --force` must NEVER auto-approve via the RALPH_SAFE `startsWith` on the leading verb — isSingleCommand rejects the `&&`, falling through to GIT_BLOCKED's whole-string match on the trailing `git push --force` — still a hard deny.
